body_txt: Zzzzz... zzzzz... zzzzz... GAH! OH NO, I FORGOT THE TICKETS!! Oh! Um... I mean... Describe Waking Non-Sequitur Here . Alice is in a coma, is sleeping or is otherwise unconscious. Upon waking, a non sequitur bursts from her lips. There's a chance that she mistakes the closest person as someone else during such. The obvious implication is that the character was having a dream at the time, and since they haven't fully woken up yet, they haven't yet realized that it was in fact a dream. Compare Talking in Your Sleep and Noodle Implements (when the outburst is implied to relate to a dream). The inverse is Non Sequitur, *Thud* , where a character says something random just before going down for the— BONK! So long, folks. Time to play the spaghetti harp. Can overlap with Un-Paused . If a character is suffering from Post-Op Ditziness , then expect this trope to ensue. Not to be confused with a Walking Non Sequitur who just talks like this all the time. If the non-sequitur is said while snapping out of a fantasy rather than a dream, it's Acting Out a Daydream .
